suppress.notes: Reviewed 2026-04-01. CVE-2025-15104 relates to nu.validator:validator, which is not present in this repo's runtime classpath. Keep this suppression only for Dependency-Check false positives where validator libraries are mapped to the generic CPE "validator:validator". Runtime review found org.hibernate.validator:hibernate-validator:9.0.1.Final and com.networknt:json-schema-validator:1.5.9, but no nu.validator:validator dependency.

Reviewed 2026-05-22. CVE-2025-21199 applies to Microsoft Azure Agent Installer products under the CPE family cpe:2.3:a:microsoft:azure_agent:* and the NVD affected-software entries are for the "backup" and "site_recovery" products. This repo contains the Java libraries com.microsoft.azure:applicationinsights-core and com.microsoft.azure:applicationinsights-web, which are being mapped to the generic azure_agent CPE by Dependency-Check. Keep this suppression only for that false-positive CPE match.

Reviewed 2026-06-26. User-service uses Azure Blob Storage only for reading the role-mapping file via BlobServiceClientBuilder.connectionString(...), then reading blob properties and content. Current runtime artifacts flagged for CVE-2026-33117 are: - com.azure:azure-core:1.58.1 - com.azure:azure-core-http-netty:1.16.5 - com.azure:azure-json:1.5.1 The opal-common-lib transitive applicationinsights-spring-boot-starter path has been excluded, so this suppression now covers only the direct Azure Storage SDK runtime path. Local dependency review still found no newer GA Azure Storage SDK chain available in the current resolved Gradle cache used by this repo. No public MSRC/NVD advisory detail was found during review, so this remains treated as a narrow dependency-check/CPE match for this repo's limited Azure Blob Storage usage. Risk is considered low for this service because the affected Azure SDK path is used only by role-mapping file retrieval from configured blob storage, not for processing arbitrary user-supplied Azure SDK requests or exposing an Azure SDK endpoint. This suppression expires in mid-September so the dependency-check failure returns unless an upstream GA fix, public advisory detail or a better remediation path is available.
